title: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Function Mapping Completeness for Each Outsourced Arrangement

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.33: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Function Mapping Completeness for Each Outsourced Arrangement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.33' running on dora_outsourcing_register.csv (contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup dora_outsourcing_register.csv
| eval fc_len=len(function_codes)
| eval validated=strptime(last_validated,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where fc_len<5 OR isnull(function_codes) OR validated<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")
| table contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated
description: Registers must reflect which functions depend on each provider. Flagging contracts without mapped internal business functions prevents hollow register entries that fail supervisory scrutiny during inspections.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GRC export via DB Connect, `inputlookup`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_outsourcing_register.csv` (contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define allowed `function_codes` taxonomy; (2) workflow assigns business owner per contract; (3) integrate procurement go-live events; (4) quarterly validation campaign; (5) tie to BIA criticality.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_outsourcing_register.csv
| eval fc_len=len(function_codes)
| eval validated=strptime(last_validated,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where fc_len<5 OR isnull(function_codes) OR validated<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")
| table contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Outsourcing Registers — Function Mapping Completeness for Each Outsourced Arrangement** — Registers must reflect which functions depend on each provider. Flagging contracts without mapped internal business functions prevents hollow register entries that fail supervisory scrutiny during inspections.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_outsourcing_register.csv` (contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GRC export via DB Connect, `inputlookup`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fc_len** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **validated** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where fc_len<5 OR isnull(function_codes) OR validated<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: contract_id, fc_len, function_codes, last_validated, provider_id, validated.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (incomplete), Donut (validation age), Single value (contracts to fix).
